content: We can frame Self-Refine’s iterative self-improvement workflow as the base structural skeleton for LLM self-reflection mechanisms, by swapping its single-perspective self-feedback step for the multiple inconsistent solving perspectives explored in recent self-reflection research. This hybrid framework would retain Self-Refine’s key advantage of requiring no additional training or supervised data, while addressing its common limitation of getting stuck in local feedback loops caused by homogeneous single-perspective critique.
